commit.message:
Add parallel subagents and scheduled autonomous agents

Parallel subagents:
- /obsidian-save spawns per-type agents (people, projects, tasks, decisions, ideas)
- /obsidian-health spawns per-category agents (links, duplicates, frontmatter, staleness, orphans)
- /obsidian-recap spawns one agent per daily note for parallel reading
- /obsidian-init spawns agents for dashboard, templates, boards, and note samples simultaneously

Scheduled autonomous agents:
- obsidian-morning: daily 8am — creates daily note, surfaces overdue tasks
- obsidian-nightly: daily 10pm — closes out the day, appends summary
- obsidian-weekly: Fridays 6pm — generates weekly review note
- obsidian-health-check: Sundays 9pm — runs health check, saves report only

README updated with new Features entries and Scheduled Agents section with setup instructions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: AuthGate was redirecting all patient tabs back to dashboard

The inAuthFlow check matched all /patient/* routes, so navigating to
Bookings, Records, Pharmacy, or Profile immediately triggered a redirect
back to the home tab. Now inAuthFlow only matches the splash screen and
login/OTP screens, and adds proper redirect-to-login for expired tokens
on protected routes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: handle Windows Terminal trailing whitespace stripping in paste events (#58)

Windows Terminal strips trailing whitespace from each line and trailing
newlines when forwarding clipboard content via bracketed paste. This
caused _on_paste to treat the paste as external, overwriting the local
clipboard (losing trailing spaces) and resetting the line-copy flag
(breaking VS Code line-paste behavior).

Use a trailing-whitespace-tolerant comparison (rstrip per line after
rstrip on the whole string) so that whitespace-only differences
preserve the local clipboard and line-copy state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Overhaul scripts, add experiment tracking, and document organization

Scripts:
- Replace nemo_sif_exec/shell and vllm_v012_exec/shell with unified
  nemo_sif.sh and vllm_sif.sh (no-args = interactive shell, args = exec)
- Remove vllm patch file binds (patches didn't work)
- Remove old Option A pytorch-devel SIF scripts (build_sif, sif_exec,
  sif_shell, eval_slurm, train_slurm, install_deps, setup_and_eval)
- Update all references across SLURM scripts, Python files, and docs

Eval:
- Switch evaluation to vLLM offline inference (run_eval_vllm.py)
- Remove old run_eval.py (transformers-based, superseded)
- Auto-name preds files with SLURM_JOB_ID or timestamp prefix

Experiment tracking:
- Add --experiment flag to train.py, train_grpo.py, run_eval_vllm.py
- Prefixes all artifact names: nemotron-lora/<exp>_<job_id>/,
  preds/<exp>_<job_id>_*.json
- Auto-writes JSON manifest to experiments/<exp>/ on completion
  (adapter path, accuracy scores, config snapshot)
- Add experiments/ to .gitignore

Docs:
- Add CLAUDE.md with full architecture, common commands, experiment
  best practices, eval config reference, and project layout
- Add EXPERIMENTS.md and RESULTS.md (experiment log + results table)
- Add GRPO trainer (train_grpo.py), prompt strategy hints (prompts.py)
- Add configs: base.yaml updates, eval_subset.json, eval_subset_by_qtype.json

commit.message:
perf: fix poll cache (was dead code) and prevent optimistic update flicker

Poll cache fix:
- getWorkshopPollData returned before pollCache.set() — cache never worked
- Fix: assign to variable first, then cache, then return
- Add invalidatePollCache() and call it from workshop PUT endpoint
- Reduce TTL to 2s for faster propagation

Optimistic update fix:
- Polls starting AFTER an optimistic update but returning BEFORE the
  server confirms would overwrite optimistic state with stale data
- Fix: optimistic update sets timestamp to Infinity (blocks ALL polls)
- New confirmOptimisticUpdate() unlocks polls + triggers immediate refetch
- New revertOptimisticUpdate() unlocks + refetches server state on error
- Applied to: updatePhase, togglePause, nextQuestion, previousQuestion

Result: Phase switching is now instant (optimistic) with no flicker.
Server sync happens in background without reverting the UI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Ampliar secciones existentes y añadir nuevas (Issue #7)

- Almacenamiento: añadir SAN/DAS/NAS Enterprise, campos de cabinas (marca, discos, protocolo, RAID, conexión hosts)
- Backup: software como select (Veeam, Acronis, etc.), campos condicionales Acronis, política backup endpoints
- Servidores: campos de virtualización condicionales (hipervisor, versión, host, cluster, licenciamiento)
- Nueva sección: Otros dispositivos / Periféricos (lectores, PDA, TPV, cámaras, etc.)
- Nueva sección: Licenciamiento y contratos (visión unificada de licencias)

Closes #7

feat: full-viewport court status dashboard with animations

Equal 50/50 cages/courts layout filling the viewport. Added Framer
Motion animations — staggered cell entrances, slide-in panels, ping
live indicator, animated modals. Removed CTA banner. Hidden admin

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: resolve gateway path for npx users — lint/diff/explain work without setup

The api-engine now checks multiple locations for the gateway core:
env var > ~/.delimit/server > ~/.delimit/gateway > bundled gateway.
Previously, npx users got a cryptic "spawnSync /bin/sh ENOENT" error
because the gateway path defaulted to ~/.delimit/gateway which doesn't
exist until setup runs. Now the bundled gateway in the npm package is
used as a fallback, so lint/diff/explain work immediately after npx.

Also adds clear error messages for missing gateway, missing Python
dependencies, and missing Python binary.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: set exact render dimensions on next/image to eliminate aspect ratio warning

Next.js compares rendered img dimensions against HTML attributes.
Using CSS to override creates a mismatch that triggers the warning.
Fix: set width/height to exact render size (86x32, 129x48) matching
the image's native 2.694:1 aspect ratio. No CSS override needed.

Reject corners where TL.y and TR.y differ by >20% of image height.
Previous bug: TL(42,56) TR(1039,435) — 379px Y difference accepted,
causing warped image to be completely distorted.

Add structured logging + error recovery tests → Grade B (25/40)

Structured logging (main.py):

- Plain text format for local development

Error recovery tests (test_error_recovery.py, 9 tests):

- Pipeline SFTP key missing / connection refused / empty directory
- Malformed CSV upload (non-CSV file, wrong columns)
- Analytics API missing required params

Total: 65 tests passing in 8.58s

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(powerups): use word position map for accurate cursor placement

PROBLEM: Emojis have different character lengths in JS:
- ❄️ = 2 chars
- 🛡️ = 3 chars
- ⏱️ = 2 chars
Previous +2 offset was wrong for shield icon

SOLUTION: Build wordStartPositions map while creating fullTextStream
- Tracks exact position of each word in stream (with icons)
- absolutePosition = wordStartPositions.get(currentWordIndex) + currentInput.length
- No more hardcoded offsets, handles all emoji lengths correctly

This fixes the offset sliding bar - cursor now aligns properly with text.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Dashboard: data-driven race factors + pace-RE adjusted target power

1. Race card power factors now read from StepB bootstrap (via master)
   instead of hardcoded [5K:1.05, 10K:1.00, HM:0.95, FM:0.90].

2. Target power on race cards uses pace-dependent RE:
   power = speed × mass / RE(race_pace)
   where RE varies at -0.033/min/km from reference pace.
   5K (faster) gets higher RE → lower target watts.
   HM (slower) gets lower RE → higher target watts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: corrigir bonus de garantia multiplicando valor base absurdamente

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: client corners → server (EvalBee approach)

LiveScannerModal sends 4 corner coordinates to /scan-v2.
Server uses client corners directly — no re-detection needed.
This is the EvalBee approach: client detects 4/4 → auto-capture →
server uses those exact corners for perspective correction.

Fallback: if no client corners (image upload mode), server detects.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix View History button: switch chat view immediately, load in background

Supabase client lock contention can cause the DB query inside loadHistory()
to hang indefinitely. Switch to chat view instantly on button click so
the user always sees a response, then load history with a 6s timeout
as a background operation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* EOD context: 2026-03-10

* Fix slack-read skill to read DMs by userId instead of channelId

slack_read_dm requires a userId but the skill was using channel IDs
from slack_list_dms, causing all DM reads to silently return empty.
Updated Step 1 to read DMs via people.json indices.bySlackUserId,
discover unknown DMs via slack_get_channel_info, and cache discoveries.

* EOD context: 2026-03-11

* EOD context: 2026-03-12 + 2026-03-16

Mar 12: competency matrix companion page, skills matrix feedback,
begin-day workflow, email triage, Gemini notes processing.

Mar 16: GKE image security RFC review (adversarial), AI assistant
rollout strategy draft, vibe coding training deck feedback,
Simon Crossan interview scorecard, data lead interview notes,
anonymised dev-analytics reports, email triage (200 processed).

* EOD context: 2026-03-18

AI strategy doc iterated through 3 versions (working → exec → v2
post-review committee). Futura risk matrix created from workshop
FigJam, reviewed by 4-person panel, scored 35 risks. Slack MCP
server updated to use scheduled messages for human-in-the-loop.
Lunch & Learn talking points, candidate assessments, Sonali
feedback review, engineering highlights for All Hands.

* EOD context: 2026-03-19

Mustafa Gecmen interview (Strong Yes, 21/25 — front-runner).
Jeremy Boon scorecard drafted (Yes, 18/25). 4-candidate comparison
complete. Samuel 1:1: security roadmap Draft 2 reviewed, GSM
milestone acknowledged. SSO rubber duck: direction set (Ory Growth
+ Auth0, SAML per-client). Notion companion page updated with
Sonali's feedback. AE screening questions sent to Lauren.

* EOD context: 2026-03-20

* Fix bold/italic formatting in Google Docs list items and revert Slack to postMessage

Google Docs: marked lexer wraps list item inline content in a text token
with nested .tokens array. extractTextRuns was reading token.raw (including
** markers) instead of recursing into the nested tokens. Now checks for
nested tokens on text nodes and recurses correctly.

Slack: Reverts chat.scheduleMessage back to chat.postMessage. Scheduled
messages via API don't surface in Slack's UI scheduled messages view,
making them invisible for human-in-the-loop review. Tests updated to
match new response shape (ok instead of success).

Add Supabase schema (8 tables) + migration script

supabase_schema.sql:
- 8 tables: cache, knowledge_base, channel_benchmarks, salary_data,
  compliance_rules, market_trends, vendor_profiles, supply_repository
- 16 indexes, RLS policies, auto-updated_at triggers
- cleanup_expired_cache() utility function

migrate_to_supabase.py:
- Reads all 15 JSON files from data/ and upserts into Supabase
- ~1,897 rows across 4 tables (knowledge_base, channel_benchmarks,
  vendor_profiles, supply_repository)
- --dry-run and --table flags, batch upserts in chunks of 100
- stdlib only (urllib), idempotent via merge-duplicates

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix loadHistory: remove getSession() guard causing silent early return

getSession() returns null when Web Lock is contended, causing loadHistory
to silently bail out even when currentUser is valid. The Supabase client
manages the session internally — RLS will authenticate via stored token.
Also: empty history now always switches to chat view so button always
produces a visible result.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add .env.testing and expand phpunit.xml <server> tags for full test isolation

- Add .env.testing with safe test-only values (no secrets) as defense-in-depth
  layer alongside phpunit.xml <server> tags. Laravel loads it when APP_ENV=testing.

- Expand <server> tags to cover ALL test-overridden variables, not just DB.
  Docker injects vars like CACHE_STORE=database, QUEUE_CONNECTION=database,
  AWS_BUCKET=spodial-workspaces into $_SERVER. Without <server> overrides,
  Dotenv's ServerConstAdapter reads these production values in tests.
